studyGuide.title: Introduction
studyGuide.content: Life processes are the basic jobs that every living thing must do constantly to stay alive, like breathing, eating, and removing waste.

studyGuide.title: Nutrition (Eating)
studyGuide.content: **Nutrition** is taking in food. Plants make their own food using sunlight. Animals and humans must eat other plants or animals.

studyGuide.title: Respiration (Breathing)
studyGuide.content: **Respiration** is breaking down food to release energy using Oxygen. It gives our body the power to move and grow.

studyGuide.title: Transportation
studyGuide.content: Our heart constantly pumps blood to carry food, oxygen, and water to absolutely every single part of our body.

studyGuide.title: Excretion (Cleaning)
studyGuide.content: Our body produces harmful waste. **Excretion** is the process where kidneys clean out that dangerous waste as urine.

studyGuide.title: Important Points
studyGuide.content:
• These processes run perfectly together inside us every single second
• If even one stops, the living organism will die
• Even trees breathe and sweep water through their trunks!
